domain_assessments.key_development: OFAC's dual-track Cuba tightening and Venezuela GL revision alongside Lukoil GL 131E May 30 cliff create multiple structural enforcement boundaries ripe for intermediary adaptation.
domain_assessments.reasoning: US sanctions recalibration exposes GL boundary exploitation risks where shadow fleets and third-country intermediaries (UAE/Turkey/Malaysia) adapt faster than enforcement closes gaps. Kuwait grey-listing amplifies Gulf evasion channels for Iran-adjacent flows. Architecture persistence exceeds episodic designations.

domain_assessments.key_development: EU AMLA operational with July 2026 RTS deadline, but post-CJEU BO access fragmentation undermines interconnection effectiveness.
domain_assessments.reasoning: AMLA's technical standards trajectory is positive but BO registries remain structurally weakened by legitimate interest access model creating country-by-country opacity. PNG grey-listing exposes Pacific BO transparency deficits in extractive sectors.

domain_assessments.key_development: OFAC gatekeeper enforcement intensifies US professional accountability while PNG/Kuwait grey-listings expose capacity/choice deficits in Pacific/Gulf enabler jurisdictions.
domain_assessments.reasoning: US gatekeeper shift from entity to professional-network accountability is structural; PNG (capacity deficit + extractive opacity) and Kuwait (operational effectiveness gaps in DNFBPs) grey-listings confirm enabler-jurisdiction expansion beyond Tier A centres.

domain_assessments.key_development: Mexican cartel huachicol/crude smuggling designations reveal commodity-revenue diversification sustaining conflict capacity.
domain_assessments.reasoning: Cartel shift from narcotics to hydrocarbons creates persistent shadow commodity architecture with state-adjacent Mexican fuel infrastructure complicity. Lukoil/Rosneft revenue persistence funds Russian conflict economy despite GL wind-downs.

domain_assessments.domain_name: Crypto, Digital Assets, and Financial Innovation
domain_assessments.key_development: EU AMLR CASP EUR 1,000 CDD threshold and July 2026 AMLA technical standards establish dual MiCA/AMLR compliance architecture.
domain_assessments.reasoning: AMLA's CASP/self-hosted wallet controls close previous regulatory gaps; non-EU CASP engagement with EU counterparties faces cross-border compliance friction. MiCA-AMLR intersection creates structural constraint on illicit crypto flows.

jurisdiction_risk_assessments.key_finding: OFAC GL boundary recalibrations (Cuba/Venezuela/Lukoil) and gatekeeper enforcement create adaptation windows for evasion architectures.
AMLA operationalization and CASP technical standards trajectory offset BO access fragmentation.
FATF grey-listing confirms BO opacity and extractive-sector enabler capacity deficits.
Grey-listing exposes DNFBP/exchange house operational gaps enabling Iran-adjacent evasion.
Post-Maduro GL revision creates shadow-fleet repositioning risk amid cartel commodity convergence.
Repression EO tightens state-apparatus designations amid non-US correspondent channel exposure.
Huachicol/crude smuggling architecture reveals state-adjacent cartel commodity revenue persistence.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: Lukoil GL 131E May 30 cliff creates structural enforcement moment for energy-sector evasion architecture.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: GL wind-down deadline exposes non-US entities to retroactive blocking; Venezuela dark-fleet repositioning creates secondary absorption capacity for Russian crude. Rosneft/Lukoil subsidiary networks persist as state-adjacent evasion infrastructure.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: AMLA operational with July 2026 RTS deadline following February consultations launch.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: EBA mandate transfer complete; technical standards trajectory on track despite BO access fragmentation. 2026 preparation year critical ahead of 2027 AMLR applicability.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: Kuwait/PNG added February 2026; total now 22 jurisdictions under monitoring.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: No removals this cycle; Algeria/Namibia approach potential June delisting but Pacific/Gulf enabler gaps expand list. Next plenary June 2026.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: EU post-CJEU legitimate interest model fragments BO access despite AMLA supervisory convergence.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: AMLD6 interconnection objective undermined by country-by-country processes; PNG grey-listing confirms Pacific extractive BO opacity persistence.

standing_tracker_synthesis.key_development: EU AMLR CASP EUR 1,000 threshold and self-hosted wallet controls due July 2026.
standing_tracker_synthesis.reasoning: AMLA/MiCA dual-regulation closes previous gaps; non-EU CASP cross-border friction increases. Enforcement pacing innovation constraint trajectory.

severity_reviews.reasoning: State-adjacent Russian energy revenue persistence funds conflict economy; May 30 cliff creates systemic blocking exposure across non-US jurisdictions.
Dual-track recalibration creates third-country intermediary adaptation windows; shadow fleet repositioning confirmed strategic consequence.
Post-CJEU legitimate interest model undermines interconnection despite supervisory convergence; persistent EU corporate opacity enabler.
Professional network accountability shift closes historical under-enforcement vector; non-US gatekeeper jurisdictions lag.
Pacific/Gulf enabler jurisdictions expose extractive evasion (PNG) and Iran-adjacent DNFBP gaps (Kuwait); EDD triggers amplify compliance friction.

cross_monitor_flags.summary: Lukoil/Rosneft revenue persistence and Venezuelan crude shadow infrastructure sustain conflict economies.
Venezuela dark-fleet repositioning post-GL revision creates Russian crude absorption capacity.
Cuban repression apparatus and Mexican huachicol state-adjacency confirm state-capture dimensions.
AMLA supervisory convergence reviews will expose member-state BO enforcement divergences.
Lukoil blocking and Venezuela crude re-entry create energy market macro signals.

jurisdiction_risk_movements.basis: GL recalibration/gatekeeper enforcement create evasion adaptation windows
FATF grey-listing confirms BO/extractive enabler deficits

Kuwait exchange house/DNFBP gaps enable regional conflict-finance flows

analyst_briefing: FIM Week of 12 May 2026: US sanctions architecture adapts via Cuba repression tightening + Venezuela GL recalibration, creating third-country intermediary exploitation windows (D1 HIGH). Lukoil GL 131E May 30 cliff represents CRITICAL Russian energy evasion enforcement moment (T1 adapting). EU AMLA operational trajectory positive (D2/D5 contracting) but BO fragmentation persists; PNG/Kuwait grey-listings expand Pacific/Gulf enabler architectures (D3 expanding, T3 worsening). Mexican cartel commodity diversification confirms conflict-finance persistence (D4 adapting). Tier A jurisdictions (US/EU) show enforcement dominance but GL boundaries expose adaptation risks; PNG/Kuwait confirm capacity/choice enabler deficits. Severity stable across CRITICAL/HIGH schemes. Cross-monitor: SCEM/ERM HIGH urgency on Lukoil/Venezuela shadow persistence; ESA flags AMLA member-state convergence reviews. Tooling ready: 4 jurisdiction movements, 3 active schemes, May/July regulatory cliffs mapped. Architecture signals dominate episodic enforcement.
